raw_consumer_statement:
American express posted a charge of {$1000.00} ( XXXX XXXX ) for a XXXX XXXX  taxi ride ( XXXX minutes ) in XXXX XXXX XXXX XX/XX/2026. Original agreed upon fare price was XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

Since the transaction took place, I have provided all my documents to American Express and spoken with them XXXX times. They have re-opened the case multiple times and taken the side of the fraudulent merchant. I informed American express of the fruadulent charge within minutes of it posting on my card as the amount was grossly higher. 

The taxi merchant charged XXXX times higher than the agreed fare and is not consistent with any legitimate transportation pricing. The transaction was reported immediately to American Express. American Express should be able to verify this in their records. 

The merchant has provided forged signatures. Please review the full dispute packet for evidence and further analysis of the case. 

Transaction Summary : Pickup : Near XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX / XXXX ( XXXX area ) Drop-off : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Distance : XXXX km Duration : XXXX minutes Fare Comparison : Quoted fare : XXXX XXXX ( ~ {$10.00} XXXX  ) Typical fare : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX : ~ {$6.00} XXXX  ( XXXX XXXX ) Actual charge : XXXX XXXX  ( ~ {$1000.00} XXXX  ) Authorization Concerns The amount charged was not disclosed or authorized at the time of payment The merchant claims contactless authorization but provides inconsistent and unreliable documentation XXXX signatures submitted by the merchant : Do not match each other, and Do not match the cardholders verified drivers license signature These discrepancies indicate the transaction was not authorized Immediate Action Taken The charge was reported to American Express immediately upon posting A notarized affidavit ( Exhibit D ) confirms : The agreed fare Lack of authorization Real-time reporting of the fraudulent charge
